hypothesis: If increasing encoder size and decoder depth improves CHROMA's ability to translate natural-language prompts into accurate multilayer designs, then larger models (1B/3B/8B encoders and 2/4/8-layer decoders) will produce designs yielding lower CIE76 ΔE errors on both the TP and validation splits, because greater model capacity enhances the language-to-design mapping and generalization to paraphrases and novel optical targets.
